text: Following 24-hour fixation in 10% neutral buffered formalin, pulmonary and pancreatic tissues were paraffin-embedded and sectioned into 4-μm slices. The sections underwent xylene dewaxing and graded alcohol hydration (100%→95%→80%→70%). Nuclear staining was performed with hematoxylin (5-15 minutes), followed by differentiation using 0.5% HCl-alcohol and bluing via 15-minute water immersion. Cytoplasmic counterstaining with 1% eosin (1 minute) preceded graded dehydration (70%→80%→95%→100% alcohol). After xylene clearing and neutral resin mounting, the specimens were ready for microscopic analysis.
